IA No. 100980/2022 - PERMISSION TO FILE ADDITIONAL 
DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
 
Date : 26-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE PAMIDIGHANTAM SRI NARASIMHA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ALOK ARADHE
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Raghvendra Kumar, AOR
                   Ms. Harsha Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. Anand Kumar Dubey, Adv.
                   Mr. Ashutosh Singh, Adv.
                   Mr. Devvrat Singh, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Bhakti Vardhan Singh, AOR
                   Mr. Narendra Kumar Pandey, Adv.
                   Mr. Sauvik Singh, Adv.
                   Mr. J Tarun Kumar, Adv.
                   Mr. Ankit Khatri, Adv.
                                     
          UPON hearing the counsel, the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. This Review Petition arises out of our order dated 25.02.2022
by which the Special Leave Petition filed by the petitioner-accused
was dismissed. The petitioner was convicted and sentenced to life
imprisonment by the Trial Court under section 302 read with section
34 of the Indian Penal Code on 27.06.1983. The said judgment and
1 of 3

--- Page 1067 ---
order was affirmed in appeal by the High Court on 24.12.2021. The
Special Leave Petition filed against the order dated 24.12.2021 was
dismissed on 25.02.2022. Thereafter, on 22.07.2022, the present
Review Petition was filed pleading inter alia that on the date of
commission  of  the  offence,  the  petitioner  was  a  minor.  The
petitioner has also placed on record a copy of his Class 10
Marksheet issued by the Uttar Pradesh Board of High School &
Intermediate Education to make good his pleading. By our order
dated 02.11.2022 while issuing notice, we directed that the review
petition be listed in the open court.
2. Heard Mr. Bhakti Vardhan Singh, learned counsel on behalf of
State of Uttar Pradesh. He has examined the contents of the
application as also the 10 th class marksheet issued by the Uttar
Pradesh Board of High School & Intermediate Education. He is also
of the opinion that the facts pleaded in the review petition,
coupled with the documents need to be verified in detail. 
3. Bearing in mind the settled position of law that a claim of
juvenility can be raised at any stage, we are of the opinion that
it would be appropriate if the facts relating to juvenility are
subjected to detailed scrutiny and inquiry by the Additional
Sessions Judge, Basti.
4. Accordingly, pending disposal of the review petition, we
direct the Additional Sessions Judge, Basti, to inquire into the
plea of juvenility of the review petitioner in connection with
Sessions Trial No 360 of 1982. The learned Judge shall take into
account the evidence on record and may also call for and permit
additional evidence and examination of witnesses before passing
2 of 3

--- Page 1068 ---
final orders.
5. The Additional Sessions Judge, Basti, shall ensure that the
inquiry is completed on or before 31.08.2026 and the report is
submitted to the Registrar (Judicial) of this Court.
6. List on 03.09.2026.
(KAPIL TANDON)                                  (NIDHI WASON)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             ASSISTANT  REGISTRAR

the Tribunal, the service of the petitioners was continued
by virtue of interim orders which was extended time to time.
The Miscellaneous Application No. 245 of 2019 filed by the
respondent  to  vacate  the  interim  relief  was  initially
allowed by the Tribunal, but later it was set aside in Writ
Petition No. 10514 of 2025 by the High Court. Therefore, the
petitioners  were  continued  uninterruptedly  until  the
Tribunal dismissed the Original Application No. 1144 of 2018
on 14.08.2025. 
3) The dismissal of the Original Application prompted the
respondent to issue relieving orders even prior to the
uploading of the detailed order by the Tribunal. Aggrieved
by the relieving, the applicants filed Writ Petition No.
26941 of 2025 wherein the High court kept the operation of
relieving orders in abeyance till the Tribunal uploads the
order  dated  14.08.2025.  The  detailed  Order  sheet  dated
14.08.2025 in Original Application No. 1144 of 2018 was
uploaded on 13.11.2025 whereupon the respondents issued the
final relieving order on the very same day. Aggrieved by the
Order passed by the Tribunal dated 14.08.2025, the Writ
Petition No. 35633 of 2025 was filed which was dismissed on
08.12.2025 on the first date of hearing.
4) On filing the present Special Leave Petition  vide
order dated 02.02.2026, while issuing notice, this Court
stayed the operation of the impugned orders passed by the
High Court as well as the Central Administrative Tribunal.
2

--- Page 1083 ---
SLP (C) No. 3647/2026
Pursuant thereto, the petitioners have joined, however, they
have not been allotted work nor paid the salary. 
5) Considering all the facts and circumstances, subject
to the final outcome of this special leave petition, the
petitioners who have joined the respondents, be assigned the
work and be paid salary ascertaining their presence.  
6) With the aforesaid observations, I. A. No. 87387/2026
stands disposed of.
7) List  the  special  leave  petition  in  the  month  of
September, 2026. 
(NIDHI AHUJA)                   (NAND KISHOR)

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL ORIGINAL JURISDICTION
TRANSFER PETITION (CIVIL) NO.564/2026
SUBIR ROY & ORS.     PETITIONERS
                                VERSUS
DEVI RAM     RESPONDENT
WITH
TRANSFER PETITION (CRIMINAL) NO. 237/2026
O R D E R
1. The present petitions have been filed under Section 25 of the
Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 (for short “CPC”) and Section 406 of
the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973 by the petitioners seeking
transfer of Recovery Suit bearing CS No.633/2025, titled as "Devi
Ram Vs. Subir Roy & Ors.", pending before the Additional Civil
Jude, Senior Division, Dera Bassi to the Principal Civil Judge at
Rewa, Madhya Pradesh and Maintenance Case bearing MNT125/51/2025,
titled as “Devi Ram Vs. Sonia”, pending before the Principal Judge,
Family Court, Dera Bassi to the Principal Judge, Family Court,
Rewa, Madhya Pradesh. 
2. Learned counsel appearing for the respondent – father has
vehemently opposed the transfer by contending that he is 69 years
old and he is unable to be physically present or travel and also
suffering from dementia.
3. Having gone through the material available on record and also
1

--- Page 1091 ---
the facts of the case, we are of the view that these Transfer
Petitions deserve to be allowed. Accordingly, CS No.633/2025,
titled as "Devi Ram Vs. Subir Roy & Ors.", pending before the
Additional Civil Jude, Senior Division, Dera Bassi is transferred
to  the  Principal  Civil  Judge  at  Rewa,  Madhya  Pradesh  and
Maintenance Case bearing MNT125/51/2025, titled as “Devi Ram Vs.
Sonia”, pending before the Principal Judge, Family Court, Dera
Bassi is transferred to the Principal Judge, Family Court, Rewa,
Madhya Pradesh.
4. Records shall be sent by the Court where proceedings are
pending to the transferee Court promptly.
5. A copy of this order be sent to the concerned courts for
compliance.
6. The Transfer Petitions stand allowed in the aforesaid terms.
7. In the light of abovesaid submission made by the learned
counsel appearing for the respondent, this Court permits the
respondent – father to appear virtually before the transferee
Court. 
8. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.  
.................J.
  (ARAVIND KUMAR)
.................J.
(PRASANNA B. VARALE)
NEW DELHI;
MAY 29, 2026.

1

--- Page 1097 ---
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Delay condoned.
2. The present petitions arise from a common order
passed by a Division Bench of the High Court dismissing
Appeal No.45 of 2023 (filed by the petitioner-Municipal
Corporation of Greater Mumbai) and Appeal No.347 of 2019
(filed by respondent No.2-The Empire Building Occupants
Welfare Association) respectively and thereby affirming
the order passed by a Single Judge in a suit instituted
by the respondent No.1 on Notice of Motion No.1115 of
2005  along  with  Notice  of  Motion  No.1320  of  2013
respectively.
3. The  High  Court  while  dismissing  the  appeals
observed in paragraphs 80, 81 and 82 respectively as
under:
“80. For all the above reasons, we dismiss
this appeal.  However, we once again clarify that
nothing in the impugned order or this order
prevents the MCGM or any other party, if so
entitled, from taking recourse to the due process
of law as against the Plaintiff.
81. Further, we clarify that the observations
or findings in the impugned order or this order
are only prima facie.  They should not influence
the Court when deciding the suit finally.  The
suit should be disposed of in accordance with the
law and on its own merits after  considering the
2

--- Page 1098 ---
evidence that the parties might present therein
and the law on the subject.
82. Both  the  Appeals  and  any  interim
applications therein are disposed of without any
order for costs.”
4. We  heard  Mr.  Siddharth  Bhatnagar,  the  learned
senior counsel appearing for the petitioner-Corporation,
Mr. Balbir Singh, the learned senior counsel appearing
for the respondent No.1/original plaintiff and Mr. Amit
Sibbal,  the  learned  senior  counsel  appearing  for
respondent Nos. 12 to 18 respectively.
5. We find no error not to speak of any error of law
in the impugned order passed by the High Court.
6. We take notice of the fact that the Suit is of the
year 2004.  We are informed that in the recent past,
issues have been framed. Since the Suit is now almost 22
years old, we request the High Court to start with the
recording of the oral evidence and try to complete the
Suit within a period of one year from today.
7. With the aforesaid, we dispose of the Special Leave
Petitions.
8. Pending application shall also stand disposed of.
(ANITA MALHOTRA)                         (POOJA SHARMA)
   AR-CUM-PS                              COURT MASTER
3

--- Page 1099 ---

                             O R D E R
1. We heard Ms. Madhavi Agrawal, the learned amicus
curiae.
2. Delay condoned. 
3. The learned amicus gave us a fair idea as regards
the  subject matter  of this  litigation and  also the
grievance which the petitioner seeks to redress before
1

--- Page 1100 ---
this Court.
4. We take notice of paragraph 8 of the impugned
order.  The same reads thus:
“8.Petitioner  will  swear/affirm  affidavit
verifying  execution  of  the  authority  letter
executed by Kishanlal and deposit it in the
district Court, Lucknow, accompanied by website
copy of this order.  We request the District
Judge  to  provide  petitioner  with  appropriate
acknowledgment of the deposit to be made as
required under clause-(a) in section 4 of Powers
of Attorney Act, 1882.  Petitioner then will
apply for restoration of the appeal before the
State  Commission  on  producing  acknowledgment
regarding the authority letter already disclosed
in the appeal.  We direct further, petitioner may
adopt same procedure in respect of other persons,
from whom he has obtained power of attorney for
pursuing their claims.”
5. We are of the view that the High Court has taken
due care to protect the interest of all those persons
whose claims have been rejected by the Insurance Company.
The  procedure  as  prescribed  in  paragraph  8  of  the
impugned  order  referred  to  above  will  have  to  be
undertaken.  Even after undertaking the entire exercise,
as prescribed in paragraph 8 of the impugned order, if
nothing is done by the authority concerned, it shall be
open for the petitioner to once again go before the High
Court by filing an appropriate Writ Petition.
2

--- Page 1101 ---
6. Once  the  entire  procedure  is  completed,  the
concerned District Judge shall see to it that appropriate
orders are passed expeditiously. The matter shall be
decided on its own merits.
7. With the aforesaid, this Special Leave Petition and
the pending applications stand disposed of.
8. We  are  grateful  to  Ms.  Madhavi  Agrawal  for
rendering her services as amicus curiae.

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL ORIGINAL JURISDICTION
TRANSFER PETITION(S)(CIVIL) NO(S).1784/2025
SARASWATI                                          PETITIONER(S)
   VERSUS
SOMENDRA SHEKHAR                                   RESPONDENT(S)
O R D E R
1. This petition has been filed by the petitioner-wife seeking
transfer of the petition being HMA No.906/2025 titled as
“Somendra Shekhar vs. Saraswati”, pending before the Court of
Principal Judge, Family Court, Lucknow, U.P. to the Court of
Principal Judge, Family Court, Karkardooma Courts, District
Shahdara, Delhi.
2. Sufficient cause having been made out by the petitioner–wife,
warranting transfer of the case, as prayed for by her, the
Transfer  Petition  deserves  to  be  allowed.  It  is  ordered
accordingly.
3. Consequently, the petition being HMA No.906/2025 titled as
“Somendra Shekhar vs. Saraswati”, pending before the Court of
Principal Judge, Family Court, Lucknow, U.P. is ordered to be
transferred to the Court of Principal Judge, Family Court,
Karkardooma Courts, District Shahdara, Delhi.
4. The transferor court shall forthwith transmit the record of
the aforesaid case to the transferee court.

--- Page 1106 ---
2
5. The transferee court shall make all possible endeavours first
for an amicable settlement between the parties, failing which
for  an  early  decision  in  the  matter  without  unnecessary
adjournments.
6. The respondent is permitted to attend the proceedings before
the transferee court online, except on such date(s) when his
personal presence is mandatorily required by the Court.
7. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
 
…………………………………………….J.

in WP No. 35633/2025 passed by the High Court for the State of

P.S.V. MADHAVA NARASIMHULU & ORS.                  Petitioner(s)

THE MINISTRY OF RURAL DEVELOPMENT & ANR.           Respondent(s)
(I.A. No. 87387/2026 – APPLICATION FOR DIRECTION) 
Date : 27-05-2026 This petition was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.K. MAHESHWARI
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ATUL S. CHANDURKAR
For Petitioner(s) : 
                   Mr. M Srinivas R Rao, Adv.
                   Mr. Abid Ali Beeran P, AOR
                   Mr. Saswat Adhyapak, Adv.
                   Ms. Namita Kumari, Adv.
                   Ms. Akshita Agarwal, Adv.
                   Ms. Udita Joshi, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) : 
                   Ms. Megha Karnwal, AOR
                   Mr. Muktesh Bajpai, Adv.
                   Ms. Awanitika, Adv.
                   Mr. Aaditya Thorat, Adv.
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1) Heard learned counsel for the parties on I.A. No.
87387/2026,  which  is  an  application  seeking  direction
against respondent to allot requisite work and to pay salary
from the date of joining. 
2) During the pendency of the Original Application before
1

--- Page 1112 ---
SLP (C) No. 3647/2026
the Tribunal, the service of the petitioners was continued
by virtue of interim orders which was extended time to time.
The Miscellaneous Application No. 245 of 2019 filed by the
respondent  to  vacate  the  interim  relief  was  initially
allowed by the Tribunal, but later it was set aside in Writ
Petition No. 10514 of 2025 by the High Court. Therefore, the
petitioners  were  continued  uninterruptedly  until  the
Tribunal dismissed the Original Application No. 1144 of 2018
on 14.08.2025. 
3) The dismissal of the Original Application prompted the
respondent to issue relieving orders even prior to the
uploading of the detailed order by the Tribunal. Aggrieved
by the relieving, the applicants filed Writ Petition No.
26941 of 2025 wherein the High court kept the operation of
relieving orders in abeyance till the Tribunal uploads the
order  dated  14.08.2025.  The  detailed  Order  sheet  dated
14.08.2025 in Original Application No. 1144 of 2018 was
uploaded on 13.11.2025 whereupon the respondents issued the
final relieving order on the very same day. Aggrieved by the
Order passed by the Tribunal dated 14.08.2025, the Writ
Petition No. 35633 of 2025 was filed which was dismissed on
08.12.2025 on the first date of hearing.
4) On filing the present Special Leave Petition  vide
order dated 02.02.2026, while issuing notice, this Court
stayed the operation of the impugned orders passed by the
High Court as well as the Central Administrative Tribunal.
2

--- Page 1113 ---
SLP (C) No. 3647/2026
Pursuant thereto, the petitioners have joined, however, they
have not been allotted work nor paid the salary. 
5) Considering all the facts and circumstances, subject
to the final outcome of this special leave petition, the
petitioners who have joined the respondents, be assigned the
work and be paid salary ascertaining their presence.  
6) With the aforesaid observations, I. A. No. 87387/2026
stands disposed of.
7) List  the  special  leave  petition  in  the  month  of
September, 2026. 
(NIDHI AHUJA)                   (NAND KISHOR)
   DEPUTY REGISTRAR               ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

1. This petition is filed under Section 25 of the Code of Civil
Procedure, 1908, seeking transfer of a case being RCA-A No.
31/2023, pending before the Court of Principal Judge, Family
Court, Ujjain, M.P. to the competent Court of Family Court,
Saket-District South, Saket Court, New Delhi.
2. Despite service of notice, none has entered appearance on
behalf of the respondent.

transfer are that she has to take care of her minor daughter;
and also on account of long distance she is unable to defend the
proceedings at Ujjain.

deserves to be allowed. Consequently, the Transfer Petition is
allowed.

the Court of Principal Judge, Family Court, Ujjain, M.P. is
transferred to the competent Court of Family Court, Saket-
District South, Saket Court, New Delhi.

SPECIAL LEAVE PETITION (CRIMINAL) Diary No. 32504/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated

VISHAL DHONDU MALEKAR                       PETITIONER(S)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF MAHARASHTRA & ANR.             Respondent(s)
(IA No. 168586/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE
IMPUGNED JUDGMENT,IA No. 168585/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING
O.T. AND IA No. 168589/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM SURRENDERING
WITHIN TIME)
 
Date : 01-06-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJAY KAROL
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AUGUSTINE GEORGE MASIH
(PARTIAL COURT WORKING DAYS BENCH)
For Petitioner(s) : Mr. Pulkit Agarwal, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) : Mr. Aaditya Aniruddha Pande, AOR
       UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The petitioner challenges the judgment and order dated
24.03.2026  in  CRLA  No.330/2024  titled  “ Vishal  Dhondu
Malekar vs. State of Maharashtra & Anr.” passed by the High
Court of Judicature at Bombay at Aurangabad.
2. Issue notice, returnable on 15.07.2026.
SLP (Crl.) Diary No. 32504/2026                      1

--- Page 1127 ---
3. Mr.  Aaditya  Aniruddha  Pande,  learned  Advocate-on-
Record  accepts  notice  on  behalf  of  the  State  of
Maharashtra.  Hence, service of formal notice is waived on
the respondent State. Let notice be issued to respondent
No.2.
4. Dasti service, in addition, is permitted. Let steps
for service be taken within one week.
5. Learned counsel for the petitioner(s) shall serve a
copy of the petition to the learned standing counsel for
the State of Maharashtra within a period of one week.
6. In  the  notice  itself,  let  it  be  mentioned  that
respondent is required to file a counter affidavit and
reply to the interlocutory application(s), if any, before
the next date of listing.
7. Let counter affidavit by the State be filed within
four weeks.
8. Rejoinder thereto, if any, be filed within two weeks
thereafter.
9. Let the original records be called for.  Soft copy
thereof, shall be supplied to the learned counsel for the
parties.
SLP (Crl.) Diary No. 32504/2026                      2

--- Page 1128 ---
10. Considering the facts and circumstances of the case
and the period of sentence, we suspend the sentence of the
petitioner in connection with F.I.R. No. 239 of 2021,
registered at P.S.Bodwad, District Jalgaon, awarded by the
Trial Court, affirmed by the High Court and grant bail to
the petitioner on such terms and conditions as may be fixed
by the Trial Court, pending consideration of the present
petition.
11. Further, if the petitioner possesses a passport, the
same  shall  be  surrendered  before  the  Competent
Authority/Court pending disposal of the present petition or
directed otherwise.
12. Application  for  exemption  from  surrendering  is
disposed of.

[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 25-02-2022 
in SLP(Crl) No. No. 438/2022 passed by the Supreme Court of India]
SANTRAM                                            Petitioner(s)

THE STATE OF UTTAR PRADESH & ORS.                  Respondent(s)
IA No. 249055/2025 - EARLY HEARING APPLICATION
IA No. 100983/2022 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
IA No. 100980/2022 - PERMISSION TO FILE ADDITIONAL 
DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
 
Date : 26-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE PAMIDIGHANTAM SRI NARASIMHA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ALOK ARADHE
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Raghvendra Kumar, AOR
                   Ms. Harsha Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. Anand Kumar Dubey, Adv.
                   Mr. Ashutosh Singh, Adv.
                   Mr. Devvrat Singh, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Bhakti Vardhan Singh, AOR
                   Mr. Narendra Kumar Pandey, Adv.
                   Mr. Sauvik Singh, Adv.
                   Mr. J Tarun Kumar, Adv.
                   Mr. Ankit Khatri, Adv.
                                     
          UPON hearing the counsel, the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. This Review Petition arises out of our order dated 25.02.2022
by which the Special Leave Petition filed by the petitioner-accused
was dismissed. The petitioner was convicted and sentenced to life
imprisonment by the Trial Court under section 302 read with section
34 of the Indian Penal Code on 27.06.1983. The said judgment and
1 of 3

--- Page 1130 ---
order was affirmed in appeal by the High Court on 24.12.2021. The
Special Leave Petition filed against the order dated 24.12.2021 was
dismissed on 25.02.2022. Thereafter, on 22.07.2022, the present
Review Petition was filed pleading inter alia that on the date of
commission  of  the  offence,  the  petitioner  was  a  minor.  The
petitioner has also placed on record a copy of his Class 10
Marksheet issued by the Uttar Pradesh Board of High School &
Intermediate Education to make good his pleading. By our order
dated 02.11.2022 while issuing notice, we directed that the review
petition be listed in the open court.
2. Heard Mr. Bhakti Vardhan Singh, learned counsel on behalf of
State of Uttar Pradesh. He has examined the contents of the
application as also the 10 th class marksheet issued by the Uttar
Pradesh Board of High School & Intermediate Education. He is also
of the opinion that the facts pleaded in the review petition,
coupled with the documents need to be verified in detail. 
3. Bearing in mind the settled position of law that a claim of
juvenility can be raised at any stage, we are of the opinion that
it would be appropriate if the facts relating to juvenility are
subjected to detailed scrutiny and inquiry by the Additional
Sessions Judge, Basti.
4. Accordingly, pending disposal of the review petition, we
direct the Additional Sessions Judge, Basti, to inquire into the
plea of juvenility of the review petitioner in connection with
Sessions Trial No 360 of 1982. The learned Judge shall take into
account the evidence on record and may also call for and permit
additional evidence and examination of witnesses before passing
2 of 3

--- Page 1131 ---
final orders.
5. The Additional Sessions Judge, Basti, shall ensure that the
inquiry is completed on or before 31.08.2026 and the report is
submitted to the Registrar (Judicial) of this Court.
6. List on 03.09.2026.
(KAPIL TANDON)                                  (NIDHI WASON)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             ASSISTANT  REGISTRAR

1. The petitioner before us happens to be the father of the
deceased.
2. He is aggrieved by the order passed by the High Court dated
10.02.2026 releasing the respondent no.1 (original accused) on
bail in a serious offence like murder. He wants that the
impugned order passed by the High Court be set aside, the bail
be cancelled, and the accused be taken back in custody.
3. We heard Mr. Nagendra Singh, learned counsel appearing for
the petitioner, Mr. Jasdeep Singh Dhillon, the learned counsel
appearing for the respondent no.1-accused, and Mr. Abhishek
Saket, the learned counsel appearing for the State.
4. We are informed that the trial is practically at the fag
end. Although, we do not agree at all with the line of reasoning
2

--- Page 1149 ---
SLP(Crl.)  No. 5750/2026
assigned  by  the  High  Court  while  granting  bail,  more
particularly, when the High Court says that one of the grounds
which appealed was, over crowding of jails in the State of Uttar
Pradesh, we would like to observe the progress of the trial.
5. Post this matter for further hearing on 16.07.2026. Trial
to proceed further expeditiously without any hindrance. 
6. We direct that the Cross Case arising from a cross F.I.R
shall also be concluded within a period of two months from
today.
7. Registry shall forward one copy of this order at the
earliest to the concerned Trial Court, i.e., Additional District
Judge/Regular, Bijnor, U.P.

Sangeeta  and  Sandeep  are  personally  present.  Sangeeta  is
accompanied by her brother, namely, Vijay and Sandeep is also
accompanied by his brother, namely, Vijay. 
3. We had a talk with both individually. Sandeep is far more
willing to save the marriage and restore his matrimonial life,
however, Sangeeta has some reservations. She does not want to
come back to her matrimonial home and unfortunately, the elder
son, is with the husband, i.e.,Samarathya,  and the daughter,
who is just 2 years old, is with mother.
4. In the past the parties did go for mediation and we are
told that mediation was quite successful. However, ultimately
the parties could not reach to any understanding.
5. We want the parties to once again go for fresh mediation.
6. This time we request Mr.S.K. Rungta, the learned senior
counsel to act as a Mediator between the parties and try to
bring around some settlement so as to see that the lives of the
two minor children is not effected in any manner.
7. We impressed upon both Sandeep and Sangeeta to save the
marriage at least for the sake of their two minor children.

CORAM :

                   Ms. Vineeta Singh, Adv.
                   
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                        O R D E R
Learned counsel for the petitioner submits
that with respect to the same premises in the other
matter preferred by the brother of the petitioner,
this Court while entertaining the SLP(C) bearing
Diary No. 32859 of 2026 titled ‘ M/s.Ram Suroop Ram
Niwas Vs. Bimla Devi’  has granted interim protection
vide  order dated 27 th May, 2026, at an enhanced rate
of rent. She, therefore, submits that the present
1

--- Page 1160 ---
matter  may also be tagged with the said matter which
is likely to be listed on 15 th July, 2026 and  till
then similar protection may also be provided to the
petitioner in the present case.
It has  also been informed that the order
dated 27th May, 2026 has still not uploaded.  
It  is  further  submitted  that  as  per  the
protection provided by the High Court, the petitioner
has to vacate the premises on 1 st June, 2026 i.e.
today.
Learned counsel further  informs that this
Court while passing the order dated 27 th May, 2026,
had provided protection to the petitioner in the said
matter,  subject to  payment  of  enhanced rent  of
 1,70,000/- (One Lakh and Seventy Thousand Only) per₹
month with effect from 1st June, 2026.
On  the  statement  of  the  learned  counsel
appearing for the petitioner, we direct that this
matter be tagged with  Diary No. 32859 of 2026 titled
‘M/s.Ram Suroop Ram Niwas Vs. Bimla Devi’.
Notice be issued to the respondent, returnable
on 15th July, 2026.
On the said date, both the matter to be
listed.
2

--- Page 1161 ---
In  the  meanwhile,  interim  protection  is
provided to the petitioner to the extent that the
time granted by the High Court  up to 1 st June, 2026
is extended.
The petitioner may not vacate the premises
till 15th July, 2026 subject to payment of enhanced
rent of  1,70,000/- (One Lakh and Seventy Thousand₹
Only) per month to be paid to the respondent on or
before 10th of every month in advance. 
List on 15th July, 2026.
On the next date, proof of advance rent be
also filed by the petitioner.

O R D E R
1. By this petition, petitioner-wife seeks
transfer of HMP No. 3003/2025 titled as “Singh
Rajankumar vs. Pragati Singh”, pending before
Family Court Judge at Ahmedabad, Gujarat to the
Family Court at Durg, Chhattisgarh.   
2. We have heard learned counsel for the
parties and have perused the record.
3. Upon consideration of the submissions and
perusal of materials available on record, we
are of the view that this Transfer Petition
deserves to be allowed. Accordingly, HMP No.
3003/2025  titled  as  “Singh  Rajankumar  vs.
Pragati Singh”, pending before Family Court
Judge at Ahmedabad, Gujarat is transferred to
the Family Court at Durg, Chhattisgarh. The
1

--- Page 1163 ---
transferor  Court  shall  transmit  the  entire
record of the case forthwith to the transferee
Court. 
4. For convenience of the parties, we deem
it appropriate to observe that it will be open
to  the  parties  to  appear  through  video
conferencing platform except on occasions where
their physical presence is necessary. 
5. The Transfer Petition is allowed in the
above terms. 
6. Pending  application(s),  if  any,  shall
stand disposed of.

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL ORIGINAL JURISDICTION
TRANSFER PETITION (C) NO.2446 OF 2025
ANKITA SIKDAR (MAJUMDER)                      PETITIONER(S)
                                VERSUS
JIBAK MAJUMDER @ JISHNU MAJUMDER              RESPONDENT(S)
O R D E R
1. This petition is filed under Section 25 of the Code of
Civil Procedure, 1908, seeking transfer of a case bearing MAT
Suit No. 86 of 2023 titled “ Jibak Majumder @ Jishnu Majumder
vs. Ankita Sikdar (Majumder) , pending before the Additional
District and Sessions Judge, Fast Track Court, Cooch Behar,
West Bengal to the Family Court, Dhubri, Assam.
2. The grounds raised by the petitioner for seeking the
transfer are that she does not have any source of income; she
has to take care of her widow mother; and also on account of
long  distance  she  is  unable  to  effectively  defend  the
proceedings at Cooch Behar.
3. On such consideration, we are of the view that transfer
deserves to be allowed. Consequently, the Transfer Petition is
allowed.  
4. Accordingly, case bearing MAT Suit No. 86 of 2023 titled
“Jibak Majumder @ Jishnu Majumder vs. Ankita Sikdar (Majumder) ,
pending before the Additional District and Sessions Judge, Fast
Track Court, Cooch Behar, West Bengal  is transferred  to the
Family Court, Dhubri, Assam.
1

--- Page 1169 ---
5. The transferor Court shall forward the records pertaining
to the said case to the transferee Court, as expeditiously as
possible.
6. Parties are directed to appear before the concerned Court
on 13th July, 2026.
7. The  respondent-husband  is  permitted  to  attend  the
proceedings virtually, unless his presence is required by the
trial Court. 
8. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
...............................J.
(SANJAY KAROL)          
...............................J.
  (SATISH CHANDRA SHARMA)    
NEW DELHI;
MAY 21, 2026

primarily concerned with the utilisation of the funds, both
for the running of the institutions and for other financial

--- Page 1173 ---
3
expenses like payment of salraies of the teaching and non-
teaching staff of the institutions.
3. Considering the submission made, we make it clear that
before spending any amount, due permission will have to be
sought for from the concerned Assistant Registrars of the
Firms and Societies, Indore, Madhya Pradesh.
4. List on 10.09.2026 after the Partial Court Working Days.
(NIRMALA NEGI)                                  (POONAM VAID)

Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  Nos.8383-8384/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  02-04-2026
in CRWP No. 6122/2025 02-04-2026 in CRWP No. 6129/2025 passed by 
the High Court of Judicature at Bombay]
SHEETAL KISANCHAND TEJWANI                         Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF MAHARASHTRA & ANR.                        Respondent(s)
IA No. 139639/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED 
JUDGMENT, IA No. 139642/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 13-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.B. PARDIWALA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K.V. VISWANATHAN
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Ajay Bhise, Adv.
                   Ms. Deepali Kedar, Adv.
                   Ms. Nupur Kumar, AOR
                                      
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The petitioner before us is a lady accused. She is a
single mother of three minor children. A First Information
Report bearing No. 552 of 2025 came to be registered against
her with Khadak Police Station, Pune on 07.11.2025 for the
1

--- Page 1217 ---
SLP(Crl.)  Nos.8383-8384/2026
offence punishable under Sections 201, 316(2), 316(5), 318(3),
318(4), 336(3), 336(4), 338, 340(2), 61(2) and 3(5) the
Bhartiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, respectively (for short "the BNS
2023").
2. The petitioner as on date is in judicial custody. She
preferred a bail application before the Sessions Court and as
the same came to be rejected, she is now before the High
Court. The next date of the hearing fixed by the High Court
insofar as her plea for bail is concerned, is 08.06.2026.
Insofar as the impugned order is concerned, the only grievance
redressed by the petitioner is that the matter has been
adjourned sometime in the month of December, 2026. 
3. We are of the view that the petitioner should pursue her
bail application which is pending before the High Court.
4. The Petitioner being a lady and a single mother of three
minor children, the High Court shall see to it that the bail
application is heard finally on 08.06.2026 as fixed and an
appropriate order be passed in accordance with law.
5. with the aforesaid, the Special Leave Petitions stand
disposed of.
6. Pending application(s), if any, stands disposed of.
  (CHANDRESH)                                     (POOJA SHARMA)
  ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH)

                   Mr. Abhijnan Jha, AOR
                                      
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Shyam Divan, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Atul S Mathur, Adv.
                   Mr. Vaibhav Mishra, Adv.
                   Ms. Saman Ahsan, Adv.
                   Mr. Prabal Mehrotra, Adv.
                   Mr. Shubhankar, Adv.
                   Mr. Raghav Bhatia, Adv.
                   M/s Khaitan & Co., AOR                          
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Mr. Shyam Divan, learned senior counsel for respondent No.1,
1

--- Page 1223 ---
who is appearing on Caveat, at the outset, has raised an objection
re: maintainability of the Special Leave Petition on the ground
that the emergency award has already been passed and the matter is
now listed before the Arbitral Tribunal on 05.06.2026. It is
further pointed out that the respondent No.1’s application for
variation of the emergency award is also pending consideration. He
submits that question of execution of the emergency award will
arise after their application for variation is decided by the
Tribunal. He, nonetheless, does not dispute the fact that the
emergency award is otherwise binding on the parties.
2. In this view of the matter, we are not inclined to entertain
the  Special  Leave  Petition.  The  parties  shall  raise  their
respective contentions before the learned Arbitral Tribunal on the
date fixed and/or otherwise.
3. The Special Leave Petition stands disposed of, along with
pending application(s), if any.
(NITIN TALREJA)                              (PREETHI DILEEP KUMAR)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        ASSISTANT REGISTRAR
2

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL APPELLATE JURISDICTION 
Civil Appeal No(s). 15121/2025
RCC E-CONSTRUCT PVT. LTD.                          Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
J. RAMKUMAR & ORS.                                 Respondent(s)
O R D E R
1. Heard learned Counsel for the parties.
2. By the order impugned the National
Company Law Appellate Tribunal (for short
NCLAT)  has  dismissed  the  appeal  of  the
appellant against the order of Adjudicating
Authority rejecting the application seeking
recall of the order dated 24.3.2025. As the
order  dated  24.3.2025  was  passed  after
hearing  the  appellant,  the  Adjudicating
Authority found that its recall was not
permissible and consequently it rejected the
recall application.  NCLAT dismissed the
appeal  upon  finding  that  no  ground  for
recall was made out.   
3. We do not find any error in the view
taken by NCLAT.  Consequently, this appeal
is dismissed. 
4. Pending application(s), if any, shall
stand disposed of.
………………………………………………………………………..J
[MANOJ MISRA]
………………………………………………………………………..J
[MANMOHAN]
New Delhi;
May 29, 2026

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL APPELLATE JURISDICTION
CIVIL APPEAL NO(S). 4003/2012
K. KANIMOZHI(MINOR) THR. FATHER                   Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
THE DIRECTOR OF MEDICAL EDUCATION & OTHERS       Respondent(s)
 O R D E R
1. Learned counsel for the respective parties submitted that
the impugned order is dated 20.07.2011; that this Court by
interim orders dated 27.07.2011/08.08.2011 permitted the
appellant to complete her MBBS course.
2. In the circumstances, the consideration of the issues which
arise in this appeal would be wholly academic.
3. Taking note of the said submissions and also the fact that
this Court by the aforesaid interim order directed the
appellant to complete her MBBS course which she has done so,
we do not find any reason to consider this Civil Appeal any
further. 
4. Hence, the Civil Appeal stands disposed of along with all
pending applications.
5. Further,  the  observations  made  by  the  High  Court  in
paragraph 18 of the impugned judgment would no longer have
any efficacy.
…………………………………………………J.
                                          ( B.V. NAGARATHNA )
 
…………………………………………………J.
                                           ( UJJAL BHUYAN )   
NEW DELHI; 
MAY 27, 2026

         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE S.V.N. BHATTI
For Petitioner(s) : 
                   Mr. Aditya Verma, AOR

For Respondent(s) :

                   Mr. Devavrat Anand, Adv.
                   Mr. Rahul Krishna, Adv.
                   Mr. Pranaya Sahoo, Adv.
                   Mr. Alekh Apurv, Adv.                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The  learned  counsel  appearing  for  the  petitioner  has
instructions from his client not to press this petition as his
client intends to avail the remedy available to them under the
Madhya Pradesh Madhyastham Adhiniyam Adhinkaran Act, 1983.
2. If any such proceedings are initiated, the same shall be
looked into in accordance with what has been explained by this
Court in the decision titled “Umri Pooph Pratappur (UPP) Tollways

--- Page 1269 ---
2
Pvt. Ltd. vs. M.P. Road Development Corporation and Another”
Neutral Citation No.2025 INSC 907. The objection of delay shall, if
any, raised shall not come in the way of the petitioner.
3. The Special Leave Petition is dismissed as not pressed.
4. As prayed for by the learned counsel appearing for the
petitioner, the SLP (C) No.27259 of 2023 which is not listed today
is taken on board.
5. The Special Leave Petition is dismissed as not pressed, in
terms of Order passed by us in the SLP(C) No.27257/2023.
 (VISHAL ANAND)                                    (POOJA SHARMA)
DEPUTY REGISTRAR                                 COURT MASTER (NSH)

For Respondent(s) : Mr. Harshvardhan Jha, Adv.

          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
Learned counsel for the first respondent-Board of
Secondary Education submitted that these Special Leave
Petitions have been rendered infructuous owing to lapse of
time and therefore, the same may be disposed.
1

--- Page 1275 ---
In response to this submission, learned counsel for
the petitioners-Students brought to our notice the fact
that by interim order dated 09.05.2017 in Special Leave
Petitions (Civil) No(s).14720-14721 of 2017, this Court
had permitted the provisional declaration of results and
therefore, the said order may be made absolute. It was
further submitted that in certain other cases, the results
were not declared.
In the circumstances, we do not find any reason to
consider these cases on merits. 
We direct that declaration of the results to be made
absolute.  Further,  where  results  have  not  yet  been
declared, they shall be declared expeditiously within a
period of three weeks from today.
The  Special  Leave  Petitions  are  disposed  as
infructuous  as  far  as  these  petitioners-Students  are
concerned.
Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed
of.
(B. LAKSHMI MANIKYA VALLI)                      (DIVYA BABBAR)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             COURT MASTER (NSH)
2

                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (C)  No(s).  18187/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  12-05-2026
in WP No. 6373/2026 passed by the High Court of Judicature at
Bombay]
PATIL CONSTRUCTION AND INFRASTRUCTURE LTD          Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF MAHARASHTRA & ORS.                        Respondent(s)
IA No. 157554/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED 
JUDGMENT
 
Date : 29-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ARAVIND KUMAR
         HON'BLE  MR. JUSTICE PRASANNA B. VARALE
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Vinay Navare, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Deepak Bapat, Adv.
                   Mr. Yogesh Subhash Kolte, AOR
                   Ms. Sonali Bapat, Adv.                   
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Against an interim order dated 12.05.2026 the present Special
Leave Petition is filed. The grievance of the petitioner is that
against three orders appeals have been filed, which is pending
before the tribunal, whereunder the impugned orders therein have
been stayed subject to deposit of 10% of the disputed amount and
accordingly, it has been deposited and learned senior counsel for
the petitioner would contend that fourth notice which has now been
issued would cover the demand already made under the three earlier
1

--- Page 1291 ---
orders and as such writ petition came to be filed challenging the
same notice whereunder order of attachment of the bank account has
been passed due to which the business of the petitioner has come to
a standstill and in the writ petition no interim order has been
passed though these aspects have been noticed by the High Court and
as such he prays for interim order of stay of the impugned demand
notice.
2. In the light of matter pending before the High Court, we desist
from expressing any opinion on merits and we request the High Court
to consider the prayer for grant of interim relief expeditiously
and preferably on the next date of hearing.
3. With these observations, the special leave petition stands
disposed of.
4. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of. 
(RASHI GUPTA)                                   (AVGV RAMU)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             COURT MASTER (NSH)
2

--- Page 1292 ---

               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (C)  No(s).  19670/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  27-04-2026
in WP No. 16384/2026 passed by the High Court of Judicature at
Madras at Chennai]
PRAVEEN R                                          Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
UNION OF INDIA & ORS.                              Respondent(s)
(IA No. 170015/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED
JUDGMENT, IA No. 170012/2026 - PERMISSION TO FILE ADDITIONAL
DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES AND IA No. 170017/2026 - PERMISSION TO
FILE LENGTHY LIST OF DATES)
 
Date : 01-06-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJAY KAROL
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AUGUSTINE GEORGE MASIH
(PARTIAL COURT WORKING DAYS BENCH)
For Petitioner(s) :Ms. Disha Wadekar, Adv.
                   Ms. Varishtha Singh, Adv.
                   Mr. Ayyam Perumal Karthik M., AOR
                   Mr. Vinayak Dubey, Adv.    
               
For Respondent(s) :Ms. Archana Pathak, ASG
    Mr. S.N.Terdal, AOR  
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The petitioner challenges the judgment and order dated
27.04.2026 in WP No.16384/2026 titled “ Praveen R vs. Union of
India & Ors.”  passed by the High Court of Judicature at Madras
at Chennai.
2.  Learned counsel for the petitioner has invited our
attention to the document handed over in the Court.  It is a
Caste Certificate dated 29.05.2026.
1

--- Page 1305 ---
3. In  view  of  the  same,  issue  notice,  returnable  on
24.07.2026.
4. Mr. S.N.Terdal, learned Advocate-on-Record accepts notice
on behalf of the Union of India. Hence, service of formal
notice on respondent No.1 is waived.  Let notice be issued to
the remaining respondents.
5. Dasti service, in addition, is permitted. Let steps for
service be taken within one week.
6. In the notice itself, let it be mentioned that respondents
are required to file their counter affidavit and reply to the
interlocutory application(s), if any, before the next date of
listing.
7. We further direct that if the petitioner fulfills all other
eligibility  criteria,  he  would  be  allowed  to  undergo  the
training which is to commence from today.
8. We clarify that the same would not enure any right in
petitioner’s favour, and shall be subject to the outcome of the
petition before the Central Administrative Tribunal.
9.  A copy of this order be given dasti.
(RAJNI MUKHI)                              (ANU BHALLA)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                      COURT MASTER (NSH)
2

--- Page 1306 ---

(iii) whether charge is framed or not;

(v) if the trial has commenced, the number of witnesses examined;
(vi) criminal antecedents of the petitioner;
(vii) whether any co-accused is enlarged on bail; and
(viii) any other information relevant for a decision on the special leave
petition.
4. Rejoinder affidavit, if any, be filed within one week thereafter.
  (RASHMI DHYANI PANT)                       (SUDHIR KUMAR SHARMA)
 ASST. REGISTRAR-CUM-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH)

respondent no.2.

in digital form to the learned amicus curiae within a period of
three (03) working days.
4. Learned amicus curiae shall endeavour to get in touch with
respondent no.2 directly, in writing, apprising her  of the
pendency of the present petition; the passing of the present
order; her right to engage a counsel of her choice; and her right
to be represented through a legal aid counsel, subject to
eligibility.

Chairperson of the concerned District Legal Services Authority,
who shall have the respondent no.2 apprised of  her aforesaid
rights.
6. Learned amicus curiae shall file the compliance affidavit
before the next date of listing. 
7. It  is  clarified  that  the  learned  amicus  curiae shall
continue  to  assist  the  Court,  even  if  respondent  no.2  is
represented by a duly engaged counsel.

forthwith.
9. The Registry shall print the name of the learned  amicus
curiae in the cause list.
10. List on 16.07.2026.

       UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
IA NO.158073/2026 IN CRL. APPEAL NO.3379/2025:
1. The  present  application  has  been  filed  by  the
appellant for suspension of sentence and grant of interim
bail.
1

--- Page 1431 ---
2. We have heard learned counsel appearing for both
sides and perused the material available on record.
3. Considering the totality of circumstances and also
the fact that the appellant has undergone sentence for
more than seven (07) years (approximate), we suspend the
sentence imposed by the Trial Court in connection with
Sessions Trial No.40 of 2018 and release the appellant on
interim bail on the appropriate terms and conditions as
may be fixed by the Trial Court. Ordered accordingly.
However, in the attending facts, one of the conditions
shall necessarily be that if the appellant possesses a
passport,  the  same  shall  be  deposited  before  the
Competent Authority/Court, till the conclusion of the
Trial or directed otherwise.
4. To comply with this order, the appellant shall be
produced before the Trial Court forthwith.
5. The appellant shall not misuse the liberty in any
manner and would maintain good conduct.
6. Accordingly, the present I.A. is allowed.
7. Hearing in the appeal is expedited.
  
     (SOURAV PAL)                         (ANU BHALLA)
SENIOR PERSONAL ASSISTANT              COURT MASTER (NSH)

IA No. 110236/26 in SLP (C) No. 16277/25 @ D. No. 22150/25
(Ekpan Associates Vs. State of WB) – For withdrawal of case 
I.A. No. 93876/26 in SLP (C) No. 20110/25 @ D. No. 38136/25
(Howrah Gases Limited vs State of West Bengal) – for withdrawal
of case 
IA No. 71772/26 in SLP (C) No. 16168/25 @ D. No. 21880/25 
(Everest Aluminium Pvt Ltd Vs. State of WB) – For withdrawal of
case  
1. The learned counsel appearing for the petitioners have
instructions from his client not to press these petition and
withdraw the same as his client want(s) to avail the remedy
under the  West Bengal Sales Tax (Settlement of Dispute) Act,
1999 as amended by the West Bengal Sales Tax (Settlement of
Dispute) (Amendment) Act, 2025. 
2. We permit the petitioners to go before the competent
authority  under  the  Amendment  Act  of  2025  and  avail  the
appropriate relief.
3. If the petitioners apply with the competent authority of
the State under the Amendment Act, 2025, the State shall allow
the applications in terms of the Amendment Act and accept the
application filed by the petitioners.
4. In view of the aforesaid, the petitions stand dismissed as
not pressed.

18.05.2026 in CRM-M No.520 of 2026 titled “ Charanjit Singh vs.
State of Punjab ” passed by the High Court of Punjab and
Haryana at Chandigarh.

notice on behalf of the State of Punjab.  Hence, the formal
service of notice on the respondent-State is waived. 
1

--- Page 1443 ---
4. Learned counsel for the petitioner to serve a copy of the
present petition to the learned counsel for the respondent-
State within a period of one week.

thereafter.

shall  necessarily  be  that  if  the  petitioner  possesses  a

Authority/Court, until the conclusion of the Trial or unless

such dates as he may be required.

all respects, the petitioner would fully cooperate, and if the

IN

1. Applications for oral hearing is rejected.
2. Having perused the Review Petition, we find that there is
no error apparent on the face of the record. No case for
review under Order XLVII Rule 1 of the Supreme Court Rules
2013 has been established.
3.  The Review Petition is, therefore, dismissed 
...........................J.
  (PAMIDIGHANTAM SRI NARASIMHA)
...........................J.
 (MANOJ MISRA)
NEW DELHI;
MAY 20, 2026.

INDIAN BANK & ORS.                                 Petitioner(s)

CHET LAL MAHTO & ORS.                              Respondent(s)
Date : 26-05-2026 This petition was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE PAMIDIGHANTAM SRI NARASIMHA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ALOK ARADHE
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Brijesh Kumar Tamber, AOR
                   Mr. Vinay Singh Bist, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Siddharth Mittal, AOR
                   Mr. Abhijeet Varshney, Adv.

                   Mr. Pramod Kumar Goel, Adv.
                   Ms. Pratiksha Bharti, Adv.
                   Ms. Akansha Bharti, Adv.
                                     
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. This special leave petition is filed against the judgment and
order dated 05.06.2025 in LPA No. 387 of 2018 passed by the High
Court of Jharkhand at Ranchi.
2. Pursuant to our order dated 07.08.2025, we are informed that
the petitioner-Bank has deposited Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten
Lakhs) before this Court which the Division Bench of the High Court
had directed in view of the damages for the negligent act of the
1 of 2

--- Page 1447 ---
petitioner-Bank. 
3. In the facts and circumstances of the case, we are of the
opinion that the compensation of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs)
along with accrued interest is sufficient to cover all claims of
the contesting respondent no. 1. In this view of the matter, the
further  direction  of  the  High  Court  enabling  the  contesting
respondent no. 1 to move the Civil Court is unnecessary. The said
observation is hereby set aside. The amount deposited shall be
treated as full and final settlement between the parties. The
Registry is directed to contact the contesting respondent no. 1 and
transfer the amount in his favour. 
4. With these directions, the Special Leave Petition and the
accompanying interlocutory application(s), if any, stand disposed
of.  
5. We  place  on  record  our  appreciation  for  the  assistance
rendered by Mr. Siddharth Mittal, learned counsel as an amicus
curiae. 
(KAPIL TANDON)                                  (NIDHI WASON)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             ASSISTANT  REGISTRAR

miscellaneous application, since it has not been filed

the subsequent would not render non-appealable.  It is to
observe  that  the  issue  of  maintainability  of
Miscellaneous  Application  has  been  decided  by  the

2
Vs. Adani Ports And Special Economic Zone Ltd & Ors.”,
pronounced on 12.05.2026 (2026 INSC 483). 
2. In  our  view,  the  present  Miscellaneous
Application squarely falls within the parameters
of the judgment of  Fakir Mamad Suleman Sameja
(supra). Accordingly, it is dismissed as not
maintainable.  Since, amicus curiae appeared and
rendered  valuable  assistance,  therefore,  we
acknowledge the contribution for his service in
this matter.

of.

1) Being aggrieved by an order dated 02.04.2026 passed by

liberty to raise objection regarding maintainability.

1

--- Page 1463 ---
SLP (C) No. 18545/2026
of such application without having any order on docket, the
Court is proceeding to decide the case on merits.

filed, it has not been decided by passing appropriate order
even on docket.  Therefore, at this stage, it is suffice to
observe that the petitioner shall file appropriate fresh
application under Order VII Rule 11 CPC before the Family
Court.   On  filing  such  application,  it  be  decided  in
accordance with law afresh and the order be passed in the
proceedings.

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL APPELLATE JURISDICTION
CIVIL APPEAL NO(S). 4003/2012
K. KANIMOZHI(MINOR) THR. FATHER                   Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
THE DIRECTOR OF MEDICAL EDUCATION & OTHERS       Respondent(s)
 O R D E R
1. Learned counsel for the respective parties submitted that
the impugned order is dated 20.07.2011; that this Court by
interim orders dated 27.07.2011/08.08.2011 permitted the
appellant to complete her MBBS course.
2. In the circumstances, the consideration of the issues which
arise in this appeal would be wholly academic.
3. Taking note of the said submissions and also the fact that
this Court by the aforesaid interim order directed the
appellant to complete her MBBS course which she has done so,
we do not find any reason to consider this Civil Appeal any
further. 
4. Hence, the Civil Appeal stands disposed of along with all
pending applications.
5. Further,  the  observations  made  by  the  High  Court  in
paragraph 18 of the impugned judgment would no longer have
any efficacy.
…………………………………………………J.
                                          ( B.V. NAGARATHNA )
 
…………………………………………………J.
                                           ( UJJAL BHUYAN )   
NEW DELHI; 
MAY 27, 2026

The applications for exemption from filing the
c/c of the impugned order and for exemption from
filing the official translation are allowed.
Delay condoned.
The application for intervention is allowed.
Learned  Senior  counsel  appearing  for  the
petitioner/complainant submit that the High Court
did not take into consideration the prior criminal
antecedents  along  with  the  rigours  o f  the
Maharashtra Control of Organized Crime Act, 1999.
Issue notice returnable on 16.07.2026.
However, we make it clear that the pending bail
applications shall be decided on their own merits
without taking umbrage of the previous orders.
(ASHA SUNDRIYAL)                                (POONAM VAID)
DEPUTY REGISTRAR                             ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

In response to this submission, learned counsel for

absolute.  Further,  where  results  have  not  yet  been
declared, they shall be declared expeditiously within a
period of three weeks from today.

concerned.

in WP No. 6373/2026 passed by the High Court of Judicature at
Bombay]
PATIL CONSTRUCTION AND INFRASTRUCTURE LTD          Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF MAHARASHTRA & ORS.                        Respondent(s)
IA No. 157554/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED 
JUDGMENT
 
Date : 29-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ARAVIND KUMAR
         HON'BLE  MR. JUSTICE PRASANNA B. VARALE
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Vinay Navare, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Deepak Bapat, Adv.
                   Mr. Yogesh Subhash Kolte, AOR
                   Ms. Sonali Bapat, Adv.                   
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Against an interim order dated 12.05.2026 the present Special
Leave Petition is filed. The grievance of the petitioner is that
against three orders appeals have been filed, which is pending
before the tribunal, whereunder the impugned orders therein have
been stayed subject to deposit of 10% of the disputed amount and
accordingly, it has been deposited and learned senior counsel for
the petitioner would contend that fourth notice which has now been
issued would cover the demand already made under the three earlier
1

--- Page 1515 ---
orders and as such writ petition came to be filed challenging the
same notice whereunder order of attachment of the bank account has
been passed due to which the business of the petitioner has come to
a standstill and in the writ petition no interim order has been
passed though these aspects have been noticed by the High Court and
as such he prays for interim order of stay of the impugned demand
notice.
2. In the light of matter pending before the High Court, we desist
from expressing any opinion on merits and we request the High Court
to consider the prayer for grant of interim relief expeditiously
and preferably on the next date of hearing.
3. With these observations, the special leave petition stands
disposed of.
4. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of. 
(RASHI GUPTA)                                   (AVGV RAMU)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             COURT MASTER (NSH)
2

Date : 01-06-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.

         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AUGUSTINE GEORGE MASIH
(PARTIAL COURT WORKING DAYS BENCH)
For Petitioner(s) :Ms. Disha Wadekar, Adv.
                   Ms. Varishtha Singh, Adv.
                   Mr. Ayyam Perumal Karthik M., AOR
                   Mr. Vinayak Dubey, Adv.    
               
For Respondent(s) :Ms. Archana Pathak, ASG
    Mr. S.N.Terdal, AOR  
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The petitioner challenges the judgment and order dated
27.04.2026 in WP No.16384/2026 titled “ Praveen R vs. Union of
India & Ors.”  passed by the High Court of Judicature at Madras
at Chennai.
2.  Learned counsel for the petitioner has invited our
attention to the document handed over in the Court.  It is a
Caste Certificate dated 29.05.2026.
1

--- Page 1529 ---
3. In  view  of  the  same,  issue  notice,  returnable  on
24.07.2026.
4. Mr. S.N.Terdal, learned Advocate-on-Record accepts notice
on behalf of the Union of India. Hence, service of formal
notice on respondent No.1 is waived.  Let notice be issued to
the remaining respondents.
5. Dasti service, in addition, is permitted. Let steps for
service be taken within one week.
6. In the notice itself, let it be mentioned that respondents
are required to file their counter affidavit and reply to the
interlocutory application(s), if any, before the next date of
listing.
7. We further direct that if the petitioner fulfills all other
eligibility  criteria,  he  would  be  allowed  to  undergo  the
training which is to commence from today.
8. We clarify that the same would not enure any right in
petitioner’s favour, and shall be subject to the outcome of the
petition before the Central Administrative Tribunal.
9.  A copy of this order be given dasti.
(RAJNI MUKHI)                              (ANU BHALLA)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                      COURT MASTER (NSH)
2

(Ekpan Associates Vs. State of WB) – For withdrawal of case 
I.A. No. 93876/26 in SLP (C) No. 20110/25 @ D. No. 38136/25
(Howrah Gases Limited vs State of West Bengal) – for withdrawal

IA No. 71772/26 in SLP (C) No. 16168/25 @ D. No. 21880/25

1. The learned counsel appearing for the petitioners have
instructions from his client not to press these petition and
withdraw the same as his client want(s) to avail the remedy
under the  West Bengal Sales Tax (Settlement of Dispute) Act,

authority  under  the  Amendment  Act  of  2025  and  avail  the
appropriate relief.

the State under the Amendment Act, 2025, the State shall allow
the applications in terms of the Amendment Act and accept the
application filed by the petitioners.

not pressed.

ITEM NO.37               COURT NO.3               SECTION XII-A
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (C) No. 18545/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 02-04-2026
in CRP No. 4268/2025 passed by the High Court for the State of
Telangana at Hyderabad]
MADANU ARUN KUMAR                                  Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
CIRRA PRIYANKA SHARON                              Respondent(s)
FOR ADMISSION 
 
Date : 27-05-2026 This petition was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.K. MAHESHWARI
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ATUL S. CHANDURKAR
For Petitioner(s) : 
                   Mr. Muddam Thirupathi Reddy, AOR
                   Mr. M. Venkata Vasudev, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1) Being aggrieved by an order dated 02.04.2026 passed by
the High Court inter alia observing that since FCOP has now
been duly registered therefore, on entering appearance by
the petitioner as respondent in the said case, he is at
liberty to raise objection regarding maintainability.  
2) It is urged before us that the petitioner has already
moved an application under Order VII Rule 11 CPC raising an
issue of jurisdiction to entertain the FCOP.  Even on filing
1

--- Page 1553 ---
SLP (C) No. 18545/2026
of such application without having any order on docket, the
Court is proceeding to decide the case on merits.  
3) It is seen that previously when an application was
filed, it has not been decided by passing appropriate order
even on docket.  Therefore, at this stage, it is suffice to
observe that the petitioner shall file appropriate fresh
application under Order VII Rule 11 CPC before the Family
Court.   On  filing  such  application,  it  be  decided  in
accordance with law afresh and the order be passed in the
proceedings.  
4) With the aforesaid observations, the special leave
petition stands disposed of.  Pending application(s), if
any, shall stand disposed of.
(NIDHI AHUJA)                   (NAND KISHOR)
   DEPUTY REGISTRAR               ASSISTANT REGISTRAR
2

Petitioner-in-person                    
For Respondent(s)  
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. After  hearing  the  petitioner-in-person,  appeared
virtually as well as Mr.Varun K.Chopra, learned  Amicus
Curiae appointed, we are not inclined to entertain this
miscellaneous application, since it has not been filed
for correction of any  arithmetic error or due to which
the subsequent would not render non-appealable.  It is to
observe  that  the  issue  of  maintainability  of
Miscellaneous  Application  has  been  decided  by  the
reportable  judgment  in  Miscellaneous  Application
No.1276/2026 titled as “Fakir Mamad Suleman Sameja & Ors.

--- Page 1559 ---
2
Vs. Adani Ports And Special Economic Zone Ltd & Ors.”,
pronounced on 12.05.2026 (2026 INSC 483). 
2. In  our  view,  the  present  Miscellaneous
Application squarely falls within the parameters
of the judgment of  Fakir Mamad Suleman Sameja
(supra). Accordingly, it is dismissed as not
maintainable.  Since, amicus curiae appeared and
rendered  valuable  assistance,  therefore,  we
acknowledge the contribution for his service in
this matter.
3. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed
of.
 
(NIRMALA NEGI)                                  (NAND KISHOR)

Ranchi]
INDIAN BANK & ORS.                                 Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
CHET LAL MAHTO & ORS.                              Respondent(s)
Date : 26-05-2026 This petition was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE PAMIDIGHANTAM SRI NARASIMHA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ALOK ARADHE
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Brijesh Kumar Tamber, AOR
                   Mr. Vinay Singh Bist, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Siddharth Mittal, AOR
                   Mr. Abhijeet Varshney, Adv.
                   Mr. Sumit Kumar Sharma, Adv.
                   Mrs. Shilpa G Mittal, Adv.
                                      
                   Mr. Sandeep Jindal, AOR
                   Mr. Pramod Kumar Goel, Adv.
                   Ms. Pratiksha Bharti, Adv.
                   Ms. Akansha Bharti, Adv.
                                     
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. This special leave petition is filed against the judgment and
order dated 05.06.2025 in LPA No. 387 of 2018 passed by the High
Court of Jharkhand at Ranchi.
2. Pursuant to our order dated 07.08.2025, we are informed that
the petitioner-Bank has deposited Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten
Lakhs) before this Court which the Division Bench of the High Court
had directed in view of the damages for the negligent act of the
1 of 2

--- Page 1569 ---
petitioner-Bank. 
3. In the facts and circumstances of the case, we are of the
opinion that the compensation of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs)
along with accrued interest is sufficient to cover all claims of
the contesting respondent no. 1. In this view of the matter, the
further  direction  of  the  High  Court  enabling  the  contesting
respondent no. 1 to move the Civil Court is unnecessary. The said
observation is hereby set aside. The amount deposited shall be
treated as full and final settlement between the parties. The
Registry is directed to contact the contesting respondent no. 1 and
transfer the amount in his favour. 
4. With these directions, the Special Leave Petition and the
accompanying interlocutory application(s), if any, stand disposed
of.  
5. We  place  on  record  our  appreciation  for  the  assistance
rendered by Mr. Siddharth Mittal, learned counsel as an amicus
curiae. 
(KAPIL TANDON)                                  (NIDHI WASON)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             ASSISTANT  REGISTRAR
2 of 2

       UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
IA NO.158073/2026 IN CRL. APPEAL NO.3379/2025:
1. The  present  application  has  been  filed  by  the
appellant for suspension of sentence and grant of interim
bail.
1

--- Page 1617 ---
2. We have heard learned counsel appearing for both
sides and perused the material available on record.
3. Considering the totality of circumstances and also
the fact that the appellant has undergone sentence for
more than seven (07) years (approximate), we suspend the
sentence imposed by the Trial Court in connection with
Sessions Trial No.40 of 2018 and release the appellant on
interim bail on the appropriate terms and conditions as
may be fixed by the Trial Court. Ordered accordingly.
However, in the attending facts, one of the conditions
shall necessarily be that if the appellant possesses a
passport,  the  same  shall  be  deposited  before  the
Competent Authority/Court, till the conclusion of the
Trial or directed otherwise.
4. To comply with this order, the appellant shall be
produced before the Trial Court forthwith.
5. The appellant shall not misuse the liberty in any
manner and would maintain good conduct.
6. Accordingly, the present I.A. is allowed.
7. Hearing in the appeal is expedited.
  
     (SOURAV PAL)                         (ANU BHALLA)

(C), 25, 27-A and 29 of the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic
Substances Act, 1985, respectively (in short, “the NDPS Act”).

of ganja.

the plea for the bail is concerned.

disposed of.

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL APPELLATE JURISDICTION 
Civil Appeal No(s). 15121/2025
RCC E-CONSTRUCT PVT. LTD.                          Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
J. RAMKUMAR & ORS.                                 Respondent(s)
O R D E R
1. Heard learned Counsel for the parties.
2. By the order impugned the National
Company Law Appellate Tribunal (for short
NCLAT)  has  dismissed  the  appeal  of  the
appellant against the order of Adjudicating
Authority rejecting the application seeking
recall of the order dated 24.3.2025. As the
order  dated  24.3.2025  was  passed  after
hearing  the  appellant,  the  Adjudicating
Authority found that its recall was not
permissible and consequently it rejected the
recall application.  NCLAT dismissed the
appeal  upon  finding  that  no  ground  for
recall was made out.   
3. We do not find any error in the view
taken by NCLAT.  Consequently, this appeal
is dismissed. 
4. Pending application(s), if any, shall
stand disposed of.
………………………………………………………………………..J
[MANOJ MISRA]
………………………………………………………………………..J
[MANMOHAN]
New Delhi;
May 29, 2026

          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Mr. Shyam Divan, learned senior counsel for respondent No.1,
1

--- Page 1701 ---
who is appearing on Caveat, at the outset, has raised an objection
re: maintainability of the Special Leave Petition on the ground
that the emergency award has already been passed and the matter is
now listed before the Arbitral Tribunal on 05.06.2026. It is
further pointed out that the respondent No.1’s application for
variation of the emergency award is also pending consideration. He
submits that question of execution of the emergency award will
arise after their application for variation is decided by the
Tribunal. He, nonetheless, does not dispute the fact that the
emergency award is otherwise binding on the parties.
2. In this view of the matter, we are not inclined to entertain
the  Special  Leave  Petition.  The  parties  shall  raise  their
respective contentions before the learned Arbitral Tribunal on the
date fixed and/or otherwise.
3. The Special Leave Petition stands disposed of, along with
pending application(s), if any.

For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The petitioner before us is a lady accused. She is a
single mother of three minor children. A First Information
Report bearing No. 552 of 2025 came to be registered against
her with Khadak Police Station, Pune on 07.11.2025 for the
1

--- Page 1705 ---
SLP(Crl.)  Nos.8383-8384/2026
offence punishable under Sections 201, 316(2), 316(5), 318(3),
318(4), 336(3), 336(4), 338, 340(2), 61(2) and 3(5) the
Bhartiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, respectively (for short "the BNS
2023").
2. The petitioner as on date is in judicial custody. She
preferred a bail application before the Sessions Court and as
the same came to be rejected, she is now before the High
Court. The next date of the hearing fixed by the High Court
insofar as her plea for bail is concerned, is 08.06.2026.
Insofar as the impugned order is concerned, the only grievance
redressed by the petitioner is that the matter has been
adjourned sometime in the month of December, 2026. 
3. We are of the view that the petitioner should pursue her
bail application which is pending before the High Court.
4. The Petitioner being a lady and a single mother of three
minor children, the High Court shall see to it that the bail
application is heard finally on 08.06.2026 as fixed and an
appropriate order be passed in accordance with law.
5. with the aforesaid, the Special Leave Petitions stand
disposed of.
6. Pending application(s), if any, stands disposed of.

                   Mr. Anuj Chauhan, Adv.
                   Mr. Shivam Bakshi, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The instant Writ Petition has been filed seeking a direction
to the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT), Bengaluru Bench to
pronounce the final judgment in Company Petition (IB) No.119/2021.
It is alleged that the arguments were heard on 21.04.2026 and the
judgment is lying reserved since then. It appears that the matter
was listed today for pronouncement of judgment, but it has now been
posted for 10.06.2026. We have no reason to doubt that the
concerned Bench of the NCLT will pronounce the judgment on the date
fixed, i.e., 10.06.2026. For this purpose, let a copy of this order
be forwarded to the NCLT, Bengaluru Bench for information and
necessary compliance.
2. The  Writ  Petition  stands  disposed  of,  with  pending
application(s), if any.

                             O R D E R
1. We are not inclined to entertain this petition which  inter
alia  seeks  the  clubbing  and  consolidation  of  certain  FIRs
registered against the petitioner and/or his co-accused in multiple
States, filed under Article 32 of the Constitution of India. The
petitioner,  however,  shall  be  at  liberty  to  approach  the
jurisdictional High Courts for the necessary reliefs.
2. The  Writ  Petition  is,  accordingly,  dismissed  with  the
aforementioned liberty.
3. All pending applications, if any, also stand disposed of.

          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R

specified regarding the directions as issued by the High Court for
consideration of regularising and the period for the purpose of
pension. It is to be noted here that in absence of an order of dies

regularised for the purpose of pension.  Learned counsel for the
respondent may seek instructions and file additional affidavit
within four weeks.

ANNEXURES)

                   Mr. Santhosh K, Adv.
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. In the counter affidavit filed by the State nothing has been
specified regarding the directions as issued by the High Court for
consideration of regularising and the period for the purpose of
pension. It is to be noted here that in absence of an order of dies
non or holding enquiry of the period of absence, it ought to be
regularised for the purpose of pension.  Learned counsel for the
respondent may seek instructions and file additional affidavit
within four weeks.

         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE JOYMALYA BAGCHI
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIPUL M. PANCHOLI
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Shubham Jain, AOR
                   Mr. Rajinder Singh, Adv.
                   Mr. Anuj Chauhan, Adv.
                   Mr. Shivam Bakshi, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The instant Writ Petition has been filed seeking a direction
to the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT), Bengaluru Bench to
pronounce the final judgment in Company Petition (IB) No.119/2021.
It is alleged that the arguments were heard on 21.04.2026 and the
judgment is lying reserved since then. It appears that the matter
was listed today for pronouncement of judgment, but it has now been
posted for 10.06.2026. We have no reason to doubt that the
concerned Bench of the NCLT will pronounce the judgment on the date
fixed, i.e., 10.06.2026. For this purpose, let a copy of this order
be forwarded to the NCLT, Bengaluru Bench for information and
necessary compliance.
2. The  Writ  Petition  stands  disposed  of,  with  pending
application(s), if any.
